description: Someone 'kithero' when they form or report a clinical conclusion from evidence under chosen criteria. The conclusion may be provisional, revised, disputed, or wrong. An exact disease label remains in its source record; the diagnosis does not decide consent or what happens next.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', pass air along it, and move into stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip and draw the tongue back into true-mid rounded back 'o'.
usage_notes: A condition or conclusion can be the object; a 'mena ... meno' clause can instead contain the diagnosed proposition. As a noun, 'kithero' means a diagnosis. Symptoms and findings may support the conclusion. Tests produce evidence, assessment compares it with a criterion, and prognosis looks towards the expected course.

examples.phi: shia mena mia ha mokanu phelu meno kithero.
examples.translation: They diagnose that I have this disease.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A sourced and revisable diagnosis cannot replace the person or speak beyond the evidence used to reach it.

semantic_domains.cognition: forming a clinical conclusion from evidence and criteria
semantic_domains.communication: stating a potentially revisable health conclusion
